protocol.name: aether_region_input_v1
protocol.copyright:

interface.name: aether_region_input_manager_v1

description.summary: manager for per-surface input region declarations

interface.description:
This global interface is the entry point for the region-input protocol. Clients bind it via wl_registry and use it to obtain an aether_region_input_v1 object for any wl_surface they own.
Through the per-surface object the client declares which axis-aligned rectangles within the surface should receive pointer/touch input ("active regions"). Any part of the surface that lies outside every active region is treated as a "dead zone": pointer and touch events that land in a dead zone pass through the surface to whatever is rendered beneath it, exactly as if the surface were not there.
This protocol is the preferred alternative to the XShape / input-shape technique because the compositor only needs to consult a small, static list of rectangles at the moment an input event arrives — no continuous coordinate recalculation is required.
DESIGN CONTRACT ─────────────── • Default (no controller created): whole surface receives input. • Controller created, no commit yet: whole surface receives input. • After commit() with zero regions: whole surface is a dead zone. • After commit() with ≥1 region: only those rectangles are live; the rest is dead zone.
Coordinates are in surface-local pixels (relative to the top-left corner of the wl_surface).

request.name: destroy
request.description: Destroys the manager. All aether_region_input_v1 objects previously created by this manager remain valid.

request.name: get_input_regions

description.summary: create an input-region controller for a surface

request.description:
Create an aether_region_input_v1 for the given wl_surface. The surface must be owned by the calling client.
It is a protocol error to create more than one aether_region_input_v1 for the same wl_surface while the previous one is still alive.

arg.name: surface
arg.summary: the surface to attach the controller to

interface.name: aether_region_input_v1
interface.description:
Manages the set of active input rectangles attached to a single wl_surface.
Changes are double-buffered: 1. Call set_input_region() one or more times to accumulate the desired active rectangles in the pending list. 2. Call clear_input_regions() at any time to wipe the pending list without committing. 3. Call commit() to atomically replace the live region set with the current pending list.
The compositor replies with the "committed" event once it has applied the new region set. From that point forward, pointer and touch events that land outside every active rectangle are passed through to the surface stack below.
When the surface is destroyed the controller is automatically cleaned up and the surface reverts to full input reception.

entry.name: already_constructed
entry.summary: a region_input object already exists for this surface

entry.name: bad_surface
entry.summary: the surface is not owned by this client

entry.name: bad_region
entry.summary: region has invalid dimensions (width or height must be > 0)

request.name: destroy

description.summary: destroy the controller and remove all regions

request.description: Destroys this controller. The compositor immediately removes all active input regions from the surface; the surface reverts to receiving input across its full extent.

request.name: set_input_region

description.summary: add an active input rectangle to the pending list

request.description:
Append an axis-aligned rectangle to the pending input-region list. Pointer and touch events that land inside this rectangle will be delivered to the surface normally.
Multiple calls accumulate: each call adds one rectangle. Call clear_input_regions() to start over. Takes effect on commit().
x, y are in surface-local pixels. width and height must be > 0; violating this is a protocol error (bad_region).

request.name: clear_input_regions
request.description:
Discard all rectangles accumulated since the last commit(). After this call the pending list is empty.
If commit() is called while the pending list is empty the surface becomes fully transparent to input (all events pass through).
Does not affect the currently committed (live) region set.

request.name: commit
request.description:
Atomically replace the live input-region set with the current pending list and clear the pending list.
The compositor sends the "committed" event once the new set is in place. From that moment input hit-testing uses the new list.

event.name: committed

description.summary: input regions have been applied by the compositor

event.description:
Sent after each commit() once the compositor has installed the new input-region set. The count field reports how many active rectangles are now live (0 means the surface is fully pass-through).
Clients may use this as a synchronisation point before drawing.

arg.name: region_count
arg.summary: number of active input rectangles now in effect
